channel.title: Leadership on Greg Herlein
channel.description: Recent content in Leadership on Greg Herlein

item.title: Your Organization Must Learn As Fast As Your Best AI adopter
item.description: Everyone is asking the wrong question. “How do we adopt AI agents?” “Which tool?” “What’s the right policy?” Wrong questions. The right question is: how does your team turn what one person learned on Tuesday into something everybody on the team uses by Friday?

item.title: The Future of Software Engineering: What I Know and How I Know It
item.description: A recent industry retreat of senior engineering practitioners published their findings on where software development is heading. Reading it felt like someone had been reading my mind — and the minds of the people I follow online — and synthesized it all into one document. When a lot of people arrive at the same conclusions independently, that’s signal worth paying attention to. Here’s my take on each major theme.

item.title: The End of the Glorified Babysitter
item.description: There’s a reckoning coming for a specific type of software engineering manager. Not the good ones. The ones who turned themselves into human Jira routers. The ones whose primary skill is translating documents into tickets and running standups. Those jobs? Gone. Faster than you think.

item.title: Where I Learned Excellence
item.description: I’ve been thinking a lot about excellence lately. Not the motivational-poster kind. The real kind – the kind that gets baked into your bones when you’re twenty-two years old and surrounded by people who simply will not accept anything less. For me, that happened on a nuclear submarine.

item.title: Foundations of High-Performance Engineering Teams
item.description: The US Navy gave me a solid foundation in leadership and operational excellence. But translating that into the business world requires a few more things.

item.title: Guest Appearance: Women in STEM Podcast
item.description: I had a lot of fun being interviewed by Aimee Vincent-Bunn who hosts the fabulous Women in STEM podcast.

item.title: Developing Leaders in the post-COVID Era
item.description: I’ve been a “leader-of-leaders” for a long time. It’s changed post-covid. But when I talk to some Engineers about the difference between “Leader of Others” and “Leader of Leaders” they don’t know what I’m talking about. So I thought I’d blog about it, and maybe even learn more myself.

item.title: Dream Teams
item.description: A friend was telling me about “Dream Teams” at Netflix and I realized that is exactly what I have been trying to build/encourage around me. That is what I want!

item.title: Guest Appearance: Software Synergy Podcast
item.description: I had a lot of fun being interviewed by James Holder for the Software Synergy Podcast. You can listen on Spotify or Apple.

item.title: September Update
item.description: Three months since I posted, so here’s some random thoughts and updates.

item.title: BrightSign!
item.description: What’s old is new again: I’ve joined BrightSign as their Head of Software Engineering!

item.title: Engineering Leadership 321
item.description: I’ve come to firmly believe that Engineering Leadership has a 3-2-1 principle to create Engineer happiness, which is a key prerequisite for high performance Engineering teams.
